skill.name: coquery-cli
skill.purpose: Run CoQuery SQL, schema detail, write safety, and JPA schema workflows from agent sessions.

wrapper_commands.name: describe
wrapper_commands.summary: Emit machine-readable skill metadata, capability boundaries, and install guidance.

wrapper_commands.name: verify
wrapper_commands.summary: Run repeatable readiness checks against a CoQuery repository.

wrapper_commands.name: demo
wrapper_commands.summary: Run a safe SQLite demo on a temporary database copy.

wrapper_commands.name: run
wrapper_commands.summary: Run one CoQuery CLI command with agent-oriented JSON output.

wrapper_commands.name: install-skill
wrapper_commands.summary: Copy this skill package into a Codex skills directory for reuse in other sessions.

notes: Smoke-proven for schema, schema_detail, query, insert, update, delete, select/count generation, and direct join generation.
Requires psycopg[binary] in the active Python environment for direct runtime use.
URI parsing exists but runtime remains a structured placeholder.
Not in the current project scope.
Entity source scanning is supported through jpa_schema.
JPQL runtime execution is not implemented.

driver_requirements.sqlite: Uses Python sqlite3 from the active interpreter.
driver_requirements.postgresql: Requires psycopg[binary] in the active interpreter, or use scripts/run_postgresql_local_smoke.sh which bootstraps .tmp/pg-venv.
driver_requirements.mysql: No working runtime driver path is exposed yet.

consumption_notes: Use JSON output when another agent needs machine-readable results.
Pass --repo or set COQUERY_REPO when the skill package is installed outside the repository.
Prefer verify, demo, or run over directly re-discovering CLI behavior.
Do not claim Oracle support in the current project scope.
